```{r, fig.width=13, fig.height=9}
                     label.y = 1015,
                     label.x.npc = 0.2, tip.length = 0.01)+
  theme_biome_utils() + xlab("") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank())

p2 <- p2 + scale_colour_manual("Sampling Strategy", values = (pal))
p2

#Simpson
p3 <- plot_richness(ps.mlf.balanced, x="Sampling_Strategy", color="Sampling_Strategy", measures="Simpson") 
p3 <- p3 + 
  geom_point(aes(color = Sampling_Strategy),
             position = position_jitter(width = 0.15),
             size = 2, alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = pal , width = 0.2, outlier.shape = NA,
               alpha = 0.5) + 
   geom_half_violin(position = position_nudge(x = 0.15, y = 0),
                   alpha = 0.25, side = "r") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = mean((p3$data)$value), linetype = 2, color= "grey") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pal) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = list(c("Self", "Professional")),
                     aes(label = ..p.signif..), 
                     method = "t.test",p.adjust.method = "fdr",
                     label.y = c(1),
                     paired=TRUE,
                     label.x.npc = 0.2, tip.length = 0.01)+
  theme_biome_utils() + xlab("") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank())

p3 <- p3 + scale_colour_manual("Sampling Strategy", values = (pal))+  theme_bw(base_size=25)+theme(
    axis.title       = element_text(size = 25),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle=45, hjust=1, size = 22),
    axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 22),
    strip.text       = element_text(size = 25),  # facet labels
    legend.title     = element_text(size = 25),
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 24),
  )
p3


figure_legend <- get_legend(p1)

figure_mlf_str <- ggarrange(p1, p3,
                    labels = c(" "),
                    ncol = 2, nrow = 1,
                    common.legend = TRUE, legend = "right", legend.grob = figure_legend)

figure_mlf_str



ggsave(
  filename    = "Alpha_div_MLF_SampStrat.tiff",
  plot        = figure_mlf_str,
  device      = "tiff",
  dpi         = 300,
  width       = 10,      # in inches
  height      = 8,      # adjust as needed
  units       = "in",
  compression = "lzw")
```




# MTS: Self vs Professionally Collected Samples (alpha-diversity)

```{r, fig.width=13, fig.height=9}
library(gghalves)
mts_df <- sample_data(ps.mts.str.2)
# Convert to data frame if needed
mts_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps.mts.str.2))

# Identify samples from each nostril
left_samples <- rownames(mts_df[mts_df$Sampling_Strategy == "Self", ])
right_samples <- rownames(mts_df[mts_df$Sampling_Strategy == "Professional", ])

# Randomly sample 33 Right nostril samples to match the Left
set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility
right_samples_sub <- sample(right_samples, length(left_samples))

# Combine Left samples with the downsampled Right samples
balanced_samples <- c(left_samples, right_samples_sub)

# Subset the phyloseq object to retain only balanced samples
ps.mts.balanced <- prune_samples(balanced_samples, ps.mts.str.2)

# Check the new distribution
table(sample_data(ps.mts.balanced)$Sampling_Strategy)

# Check the first few values of the OTU table

# comparisons
my_comparisons <- list( c("Self", "Professional"))

pal <- sampling_strategy_colors
theme_set(theme_bw())

# only significant comparison
#Shannon
p1 <- plot_richness(ps.mts.balanced, x="Sampling_Strategy",color="Sampling_Strategy", measures="Shannon") 
p1 <- p1 + 
  geom_point(aes(color = Sampling_Strategy),
             position = position_jitter(width = 0.15),
             size = 2, alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = pal, width = 0.2, outlier.shape = NA,
               alpha = 0.5) + 
   geom_half_violin(position = position_nudge(x = 0.15, y = 0),
                   alpha = 0.25, side = "r") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = mean((p1$data)$value), linetype = 2, color= "grey") +
  guides(colour = guide_legend(nrow = 1)) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pal) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +
  stat_compare_means(
    comparisons = list(c("Self", "Professional")),
    aes(label = ..p.signif..), 
    method = "wilcox.test",p.adjust.method = "fdr",
    paired = TRUE,
    label.y = 3.6, 
    label.x.npc = 0.2,
    tip.length = 0.01)+
  theme_biome_utils() + xlab("") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank())
p1 <- p1 + scale_fill_manual("Sampling Strategy", values = (pal))+  theme_bw(base_size=25)+theme(
    axis.title       = element_text(size = 25),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle=45, hjust=1, size = 22),
    axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 22),
    strip.text       = element_text(size = 25),  # facet labels
    legend.title     = element_text(size = 25),
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 24),
  )
p1



#for the observed
ps.mts.prof.obs <- ps.nasal %>% ps_filter(Sample_Type =="MTS")
table(as.data.frame(sample_data(ps.mts.prof.obs))$Sampling_Strategy)

# Convert to data frame if needed
mts_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps.mts.prof.obs))

# Identify samples from each nostril
left_samples <- rownames(mts_df[mts_df$Sampling_Strategy == "Self", ])
right_samples <- rownames(mts_df[mts_df$Sampling_Strategy == "Professional", ])

# Randomly sample 33 Right nostril samples to match the Left
set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility
right_samples_sub <- sample(right_samples, length(left_samples))

# Combine Left samples with the downsampled Right samples
balanced_samples <- c(left_samples, right_samples_sub)

# Subset the phyloseq object to retain only balanced samples
ps.mts.balanced.obs <- prune_samples(balanced_samples, ps.mts.prof.obs)

# Check the new distribution
table(sample_data(ps.mts.balanced.obs)$Sampling_Strategy)

#Observed
p2 <- plot_richness(ps.mts.balanced.obs, x="Sampling_Strategy", color="Sampling_Strategy", measures="Chao1")
p2 <- p2 +
  geom_point(aes(color = Sampling_Strategy),
             position = position_jitter(width = 0.15),
             size = 2, alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = pal , width = 0.2, outlier.shape = NA,
               alpha = 0.5) +
   geom_half_violin(position = position_nudge(x = 0.15, y = 0),
                   alpha = 0.25, side = "r") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = mean((p2$data)$value), linetype = 2, color= "grey") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pal) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = list(c("Self", "Professional")),
                     aes(label = ..p.signif..),
                     method = "wilcox.test",p.adjust.method = "fdr",
                     paired = TRUE,
                     label.y = 915,
                     label.x.npc = 0.2, tip.length = 0.01)+
  theme_biome_utils() + xlab("") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank())

p2 <- p2 + scale_colour_manual("Sampling Strategy", values = (pal))
p2

#Simpson
p3 <- plot_richness(ps.mts.balanced, x="Sampling_Strategy", color="Sampling_Strategy", measures="Simpson") 
p3 <- p3 + 
  geom_point(aes(color = Sampling_Strategy),
             position = position_jitter(width = 0.15),
             size = 2, alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = pal , width = 0.2, outlier.shape = NA,
               alpha = 0.5) + 
   geom_half_violin(position = position_nudge(x = 0.15, y = 0),
                   alpha = 0.25, side = "r") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = mean((p3$data)$value), linetype = 2, color= "grey") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pal) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = list(c("Self", "Professional")),
                     aes(label = ..p.signif..), 
                     method = "wilcox.test",p.adjust.method = "fdr",
                     label.y = c(1),
                     paired=TRUE,
                     label.x.npc = 0.2, tip.length = 0.01)+
  theme_biome_utils() + xlab("") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank())

p3 <- p3 + scale_colour_manual("Sampling Strategy", values = (pal))+  theme_bw(base_size=25)+theme(
    axis.title       = element_text(size = 25),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle=45, hjust=1,size = 22),
    axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 22),
    strip.text       = element_text(size = 25),  # facet labels
    legend.title     = element_text(size = 25),
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 24),
  )
p3


figure_legend <- get_legend(p1)

figure_mts_str <- ggarrange(p1, p3,
                    labels = c(" "),
                    ncol = 2, nrow = 1,
                    common.legend = TRUE, legend = "right", legend.grob = figure_legend)

figure_mts_str


ggsave(
  filename    = "Alpha_div_MTS_SampStrat.tiff",
  plot        = figure_mts_str,
  device      = "tiff",
  dpi         = 300,
  width       = 10,      # in inches
  height      = 8,      # adjust as needed
  units       = "in",
  compression = "lzw")

```





# Saliva: Self vs Professionally Collected Samples (alpha-diversity)
```{r, fig.width=13, fig.height=9}
library(gghalves)
sal_df <- sample_data(ps.saliva.str.2)
# Convert to data frame if needed
sal_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps.saliva.str.2))

# Identify samples from each nostril
left_samples <- rownames(sal_df[sal_df$Sampling_Strategy == "Self", ])
right_samples <- rownames(sal_df[sal_df$Sampling_Strategy == "Professional", ])

# Randomly sample 33 Right nostril samples to match the Left
set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility
right_samples_sub <- sample(right_samples, length(left_samples))

# Combine Left samples with the downsampled Right samples
balanced_samples <- c(left_samples, right_samples_sub)

# Subset the phyloseq object to retain only balanced samples
ps.sal.balanced <- prune_samples(balanced_samples, ps.saliva.str.2)

# Check the new distribution
table(sample_data(ps.sal.balanced)$Sampling_Strategy)

# Check the first few values of the OTU table

# comparisons
#my_comparisons <- list( c("H_Control", "H_ILI"), c("B_Control", "B_ILI"), c("V_Control", "V_ILI"))
my_comparisons <- list( c("Self", "Professional"))

pal <- sampling_strategy_colors
theme_set(theme_bw())

# only significant comparison
#my_comparisons <- list( c("Control", "B"), c("H", "B"))
#Shannon
p1 <- plot_richness(ps.sal.balanced, x="Sampling_Strategy",color="Sampling_Strategy", measures="Shannon") 
p1 <- p1 + 
  geom_point(aes(color = Sampling_Strategy),
             position = position_jitter(width = 0.15),
             size = 2, alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = pal, width = 0.2, outlier.shape = NA,
               alpha = 0.5) + 
   geom_half_violin(position = position_nudge(x = 0.15, y = 0),
                   alpha = 0.25, side = "r") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = mean((p1$data)$value), linetype = 2, color= "grey") +
  guides(colour = guide_legend(nrow = 1)) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pal) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +
  stat_compare_means(
    comparisons = list(c("Self", "Professional")),
    aes(label = ..p.signif..), 
    method = "wilcox.test",p.adjust.method = "fdr",
    paired = TRUE,
    label.y = 3.6, 
    label.x.npc = 0.2,
    tip.length = 0.01)+
  theme_biome_utils() + xlab("") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank())
p1 <- p1 + scale_fill_manual("Sampling Strategy", values = (pal))+  theme_bw(base_size=25)+theme(
    axis.title       = element_text(size = 25),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle=45, hjust=1, size = 22),
    axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 22),
    strip.text       = element_text(size = 25),  # facet labels
    legend.title     = element_text(size = 25),
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 24),
  )
p1



#for the observed
ps.sal.prof.obs <- ps.oral %>% ps_filter(Sample_Type =="Saliva")
table(as.data.frame(sample_data(ps.sal.prof.obs))$Sampling_Strategy)

# Convert to data frame if needed
sal_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps.sal.prof.obs))

# Identify samples from each nostril
left_samples <- rownames(sal_df[sal_df$Sampling_Strategy == "Self", ])
right_samples <- rownames(sal_df[sal_df$Sampling_Strategy == "Professional", ])

# Randomly sample 33 Right nostril samples to match the Left
set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility
right_samples_sub <- sample(right_samples, length(left_samples))

# Combine Left samples with the downsampled Right samples
balanced_samples <- c(left_samples, right_samples_sub)

# Subset the phyloseq object to retain only balanced samples
ps.sal.balanced.obs <- prune_samples(balanced_samples, ps.sal.prof.obs)

# Check the new distribution
table(sample_data(ps.sal.balanced.obs)$Sampling_Strategy)

#Observed
p2 <- plot_richness(ps.sal.balanced.obs, x="Sampling_Strategy", color="Sampling_Strategy", measures="Chao1")
p2 <- p2 +
  geom_point(aes(color = Sampling_Strategy),
             position = position_jitter(width = 0.15),
             size = 2, alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = pal , width = 0.2, outlier.shape = NA,
               alpha = 0.5) +
   geom_half_violin(position = position_nudge(x = 0.15, y = 0),
                   alpha = 0.25, side = "r") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = mean((p2$data)$value), linetype = 2, color= "grey") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pal) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = list(c("Self", "Professional")),
                     aes(label = ..p.signif..),
                     method = "wilcox.test",p.adjust.method = "fdr",
                     paired = TRUE,
                     label.y = 915,
                     label.x.npc = 0.2, tip.length = 0.01)+
  theme_biome_utils() + xlab("") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank())

p2 <- p2 + scale_colour_manual("Sampling Strategy", values = (pal))
p2

#Simpson
p3 <- plot_richness(ps.sal.balanced, x="Sampling_Strategy", color="Sampling_Strategy", measures="Simpson") 
p3 <- p3 + 
  geom_point(aes(color = Sampling_Strategy),
             position = position_jitter(width = 0.15),
             size = 2, alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = pal , width = 0.2, outlier.shape = NA,
               alpha = 0.5) + 
   geom_half_violin(position = position_nudge(x = 0.15, y = 0),
                   alpha = 0.25, side = "r") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = mean((p3$data)$value), linetype = 2, color= "grey") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pal) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = list(c("Self", "Professional")),
                     aes(label = ..p.signif..), 
                     method = "wilcox.test",p.adjust.method = "fdr",
                     label.y = c(1),
                     paired=TRUE,
                     label.x.npc = 0.2, tip.length = 0.01)+
  theme_biome_utils() + xlab("") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank())

p3 <- p3 + scale_colour_manual("Sampling Strategy", values = (pal))+  theme_bw(base_size=25)+theme(
    axis.title       = element_text(size = 25),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle=45, hjust=1, size = 22),
    axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 22),
    strip.text       = element_text(size = 25),  # facet labels
    legend.title     = element_text(size = 25),
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 24),
  )
p3


figure_legend <- get_legend(p1)

figure_sal_str <- ggarrange(p1, p3,
                    labels = c(" "),
                    ncol = 2, nrow = 1,
                    common.legend = TRUE, legend = "right", legend.grob = figure_legend)

figure_sal_str


ggsave(
  filename    = "Alpha_div_Saliva_SampStrat.tiff",
  plot        = figure_sal_str,
  device      = "tiff",
  dpi         = 300,
  width       = 10,      # in inches
  height      = 8,      # adjust as needed
  units       = "in",
  compression = "lzw")

```

```{r}

# 1. Agglomerate at Genus level
ps.genus <- tax_glom(ps.rel.oral, taxrank = "Genus", NArm = FALSE)

# 2. Overwrite the taxa_names() with the Genus labels
genus_labels <- tax_table(ps.genus)[, "Genus"]
taxa_names(ps.genus) <- genus_labels

# 3. Sum abundances and pick top-6
abundances <- taxa_sums(ps.genus)
top6 <- sort(abundances, decreasing = TRUE)[1:6]

# 4. Wrap up in a table
top6_df <- data.frame(
  Genus     = names(top6),
  Abundance = as.numeric(top6),
  row.names = NULL
)
top6_df
```

```{r}

# 1. Agglomerate at Genus level
ps.genus <- tax_glom(ps.rel.nose, taxrank = "Genus", NArm = FALSE)

# 2. Overwrite the taxa_names() with the Genus labels
genus_labels <- tax_table(ps.genus)[, "Genus"]
taxa_names(ps.genus) <- genus_labels

# 3. Sum abundances and pick top-6
abundances <- taxa_sums(ps.genus)
top6 <- sort(abundances, decreasing = TRUE)[1:6]

# 4. Wrap up in a table
top6_df <- data.frame(
  Genus     = names(top6),
  Abundance = as.numeric(top6),
  row.names = NULL
)
top6_df
```

# Boxplots: Top 6 Genera per URT Region
```{r, fig.width=14, fig.height=11}
# Convert the phyloseq object to a data frame
df <- psmelt(ps.rel.oral)

# Define your top 5 genera
top_genera <- c("Streptococcus", "Prevotella_7", "Veillonella", "Haemophilus", "Rothia","Actinomyces")

# Filter the data frame for these genera
df_top <- subset(df, Genus %in% top_genera)
df_top$Genus <- factor(df_top$Genus, levels = top_genera)


# Compute common IDs across sample types
common_ids <- Reduce(intersect, split(df_top$ID, df_top$Sample_Type))

# Filter the data for only those IDs
df_common <- df_top %>% filter(ID %in% common_ids)

# Define the comparison: adjust the group names as needed.
# If you have exactly two sample types per facet (e.g., "NPS" and "MLF"):
comp_list <- list(c("OPS", "Saliva"))

# Create faceted boxplot and add significance bracket with stars in the middle
top5_plot_oral <- ggplot(df_common, aes(x = Sample_Type, y = Abundance, fill = Sample_Type)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~ Genus) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Sample Type", values = pcoa_colors) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = "", y = "Relative Abundance (%)") +
   theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    strip.text = element_text(size = 16, face = "italic")  # bigger & italic facet labels
  ) +
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = comp_list,
                     method = "wilcox.test", p.adjust.method = "fdr",
                     paired = TRUE, 
                     label = "p.signif", 
                     tip.length = 0.01,
                     size=5, y.position = c(42, 42, 42, 42, 42, 42)   )

top5_plot_oral


ggsave(
  filename    = "Top_6_oral_cavity.tiff",
  plot        = top5_plot_oral,
  device      = "tiff",
  dpi         = 300,
  width       = 11,      # in inches
  height      = 13,      # adjust as needed
  units       = "in",
  compression = "lzw"
)
```


# Boxplots: Top 6 Genera per URT Region
Comparisons of the samples were made on the core taxa and not on top abundant. 
```{r, fig.width=14, fig.height=12}
# Convert the phyloseq object to a data frame
df <- psmelt(ps.rel.nose)

# Define your top 5 genera
top_genera <- c("Corynebacterium", "Staphylococcus","Dolosigranulum" ,"Lawsonella", "Streptococcus","Moraxella")

# Filter the data frame for these genera
df_top <- subset(df, Genus %in% top_genera)
df_top$Genus <- factor(df_top$Genus, levels = top_genera)

# Compute common IDs across sample types
df_top_2 <- df_top %>% 
  filter(Nostril=="Right"|is.na(Nostril))


common_ids <- Reduce(intersect, split(df_top_2$ID, df_top_2$Sample_Type))

# Filter the data for only those IDs
df_common <- df_top_2 %>% filter(ID %in% common_ids)
df_common <- df_common %>%
  mutate(Sample_Type = factor(Sample_Type, levels = c("NPS","MTS","MLF")))
# Define the comparison: adjust the group names as needed.
# If you have exactly two sample types per facet (e.g., "NPS" and "MLF"):
comp_list <- list(c("NPS", "MTS"),
                  c("NPS","MLF"),
                  c("MTS","MLF"))
# Create boxplots with sample type on x-axis and relative abundance on y-axis, faceted by Genus
top5_plot_nasal <- ggplot(df_common, aes(x = Sample_Type, y = Abundance, fill=Sample_Type)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~ Genus, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Sample Type", values = pcoa_colors) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = "", y = "Relative Abundance (%)") +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    strip.text = element_text(size = 16, face = "italic")  # bigger & italic facet labels
  ) +
  stat_compare_means(
    comparisons = comp_list,
    method = "wilcox.test", p.adjust.method = "fdr",
    paired = TRUE,
    label = "p.signif",
    tip.length = 0.01,
    size = 5
  )

top5_plot_nasal




ggsave(
  filename    = "Top_6_nasal_cavity.tiff",
  plot        = top5_plot_nasal,
  device      = "tiff",
  dpi         = 300,
  width       = 11,      # in inches
  height      = 13,      # adjust as needed
  units       = "in",
  compression = "lzw"
)
```



```{r, fig.width=15, fig.height=18}
library(grid)
library(gridExtra)
# combined_plot <- grid.arrange(top5_plot_oral, top5_plot_nasal, nrow = 2)
top5_plot_nasal <- top5_plot_nasal+
    theme_bw(base_size=25)+theme(
    axis.title       = element_text(size = 28),
    axis.text.x      = element_text( size = 25),
    axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 25),
    strip.text       = element_text(size = 23, face = "italic"),  # facet labels
    legend.title     = element_text(size = 26),
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 25)
  )

top5_plot_oral <- top5_plot_oral+
   theme_bw(base_size=25)+theme(
    axis.title       = element_text(size = 28),
    axis.text.x      = element_text( size = 25),
    axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 25),
    strip.text       = element_text(size = 23, face = "italic"),  # facet labels
    legend.title     = element_text(size = 26),
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 25))

nasal_label <- textGrob("Nasal Cavity",
                        rot = 90,
                        x   = unit(0.8, "npc"),   # move 80% of the way across its cell
                        gp  = gpar(fontsize =28, fontface = "bold"))
oral_label  <- textGrob("Oral Cavity",
                        rot = 90,
                        x   = unit(0.8, "npc"),
                        gp  = gpar(fontsize = 28, fontface = "bold"))

# 2) Tweak the widths so the label column is much narrower:
row1 <- arrangeGrob(nasal_label, top5_plot_nasal, ncol = 2,
                    widths = c(0.2, 0.8))   # 20% for the label, 80% for the plot
row2 <- arrangeGrob(oral_label,  top5_plot_oral,  ncol = 2,
                    widths = c(0.2, 0.8))

# Combine rows into one figure
combined_plot<- grid.arrange(row1, row2, nrow = 2)


ggsave(
  filename    = "Top_6_with_regions2.tiff",
  plot        = combined_plot,
  device      = "tiff",
  dpi         = 300,
  width       = 18,      # in inches
  height      = 23,      # adjust as needed
  units       = "in",
  compression = "lzw"
)
```


# Top 10 most abudant
```{r}
get_top10_by_genus <- function(ps, type, sampvar = "Sample_Type"){
  # pull sample data into a data.frame
  sd <- as(sample_data(ps), "data.frame")
  # subset sample names where that column == type
  samples_to_keep <- rownames(sd)[ sd[[sampvar]] == type ]
  # prune the phyloseq object
  ps_sub <- prune_samples(samples_to_keep, ps)
  
  # get ASV sums
  asv_sums <- taxa_sums(ps_sub)
  # get genus labels
  genera  <- as.character(tax_table(ps_sub)[, "Genus"])
  
  # drop unclassified
  keep    <- !is.na(genera) & genera != ""
  asv_sums <- asv_sums[keep]
  genera   <- genera[keep]
  
  # sum by genus
  genus_totals <- tapply(asv_sums, genera, sum)
  
  # pick top 10
  top10 <- sort(genus_totals, decreasing = TRUE)[1:10]
  data.frame(Genus = names(top10),
             Abundance = as.numeric(top10),
             row.names = NULL)
}

# 
top10_sal <- get_top10_by_genus(ps.rel.oral, "Saliva", sampvar = "Sample_Type")
top10_op  <- get_top10_by_genus(ps.rel.oral, "OPS",    sampvar = "Sample_Type")

top10_sal
top10_op
```


## Boxplots: Top 6 Genera per URT Region (colored by genera)
```{r, fig.width=13, fig.height=10}
# Convert the phyloseq object to a data frame

# Define top genera

# Convert the phyloseq object to a data frame
df <- psmelt(ps.rel.oral)

top_genera_op <- c("Streptococcus", "Prevotella_7",  "Veillonella", "Actinomyces","Leptotrichia","Neisseria")
top_genera_sal <- c("Streptococcus",  "Haemophilus", "Rothia","Neisseria",  "Veillonella","Actinomyces")


# Filter the data frame for these genera
df_op <- subset(df, sample_type == "OP")
df_op <- subset(df_op, Genus %in% top_genera_op)
df_op$Genus <- factor(df_op$Genus, levels = top_genera_op)

df_sal <- subset(df, sample_type == "Saliva")
df_sal <- subset(df_sal, Genus %in% top_genera_sal)
df_sal$Genus <- factor(df_sal$Genus, levels = top_genera_sal)

# Create boxplots with sample type on x-axis and relative abundance on y-axis, faceted by Genus
top5_plot_oral_g <- ggplot(df_top, aes(x = Genus, y = Abundance, fill=Genus)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~ Sample_Type) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Genus", values = top_genera_cols)+
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = "", y = "Relative Abundance (%)")+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1))+
  theme(legend.position = "right")

top5_plot_oral_g

## OP


# Create boxplots with sample type on x-axis and relative abundance on y-axis, faceted by Genus
top6_op <- ggplot(df_op, aes(x = Genus, y = Abundance, fill=Genus)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~ Sample_Type) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Genus", values = top_genera_cols)+
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = "", y = "Relative Abundance (%)")+
  theme_bw(base_size=25)+theme(
    axis.title       = element_text(size = 25),
     axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x     = element_blank(), 
    axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 22),
    strip.text       = element_text(size = 25),  # facet labels
    legend.title     = element_text(size = 25),
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 24),
  )+
  theme(legend.position = "right") +ylim(0,65)

top6_op


## Sal
# Filter the data frame for these genera
df_top_Sal <- subset(df_sal, Genus %in% top_genera_sal)
df_top_Sal$Genus <- factor(df_top_Sal$Genus, levels = top_genera_sal)

# Create boxplots with sample type on x-axis and relative abundance on y-axis, faceted by Genus
top6_sal<- ggplot(df_top_Sal, aes(x = Genus, y = Abundance, fill=Genus)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~ Sample_Type) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Genus", values = top_genera_cols)+
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = "", y = "Relative Abundance (%)")+
  theme_bw(base_size=25)+theme(
    axis.title       = element_text(size = 25),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x     = element_blank(), 
    axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 22),
    strip.text       = element_text(size = 25),  # facet labels
    legend.title     = element_text(size = 25),
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 24),
  )+
  theme(legend.position = "right")

top6_sal

# Combine rows into one figure


combined_plot_opsal<- grid.arrange(top6_op, top6_sal, nrow = 1)

ggsave(
  filename    = "Top_6_OPSnSaliva.tiff",
  plot        = combined_plot_opsal,
  device      = "tiff",
  dpi         = 300,
  width       = 13,      # in inches
  height      = 17,      # adjust as needed
  units       = "in",
  compression = "lzw"
)
```


```{r}
top_genera_all <- c("Streptococcus", "Prevotella_7", "Veillonella", "Actinomyces", 
                    "Leptotrichia", "Neisseria", "Haemophilus", "Rothia")
df_op$Genus  <- factor(df_op$Genus, levels = top_genera_all)
df_sal$Genus <- factor(df_sal$Genus, levels = top_genera_all)

# Add a column to identify sample origin
df_op$Group <- "OP"
df_sal$Group <- "Saliva"

# Combine both into one data frame
df_combined <- rbind(df_op, df_sal)

# Make sure Genus factor has all desired levels
df_combined$Genus <- factor(df_combined$Genus, levels = top_genera_all)
top_plot_combined <- ggplot(df_combined, aes(x = Genus, y = Abundance, fill = Genus)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~ Group) +  # One panel for OP, one for Saliva
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Genus", values = top_genera_cols) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 25) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Relative Abundance (%)") +
  theme(
    axis.title       = element_text(size = 25),
    axis.text.x      = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x     = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 22),
    strip.text       = element_text(size = 25),
    legend.title     = element_text(size = 25),
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 24),
    legend.position  = "right"
  )

top_plot_combined

ggsave("Top_6_OPSnSaliva_combined.tiff", top_plot_combined,
       device = "tiff", dpi = 300,
       width = 13, height = 10, units = "in", compression = "lzw")
```



## Boxplots: Top 6 Genera per URT Region (colored by genera)
```{r, fig.width=14, fig.height=10}
# Convert the phyloseq object to a data frame
df <- psmelt(ps.rel.nose)

# Define your top 5 genera
top_genera <- c("Corynebacterium", "Staphylococcus","Dolosigranulum" ,"Lawsonella", "Streptococcus","Moraxella")

# Filter the data frame for these genera
df_top <- subset(df, Genus %in% top_genera)
df_top$Genus <- factor(df_top$Genus, levels = top_genera)

# Create boxplots with sample type on x-axis and relative abundance on y-axis, faceted by Genus
top5_plot_nasal_g <- ggplot(df_top, aes(x = Genus, y = Abundance, fill=Genus)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~ Sample_Type) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Genus", values = top_genera_cols)+
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = "", y = "Relative Abundance (%)")+
  theme_bw(base_size=25)+theme(
    axis.title       = element_text(size = 25),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x     = element_blank(), 
    axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 22),
    strip.text       = element_text(size = 25),  # facet labels
    legend.title     = element_text(size = 25),
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 24),
  )+
  theme(legend.position = "right")

top5_plot_nasal_g


ggsave(
  filename    = "Top_6_Nose_Samples.tiff",
  plot        = top5_plot_nasal_g,
  device      = "tiff",
  dpi         = 300,
  width       = 13,      # in inches
  height      = 17,      # adjust as needed
  units       = "in",
  compression = "lzw"
)
```



```{r, fig.width=11, fig.height=14}

library(gridExtra)
library(grid)
# Create left side labels as grobs (rotated 90 degrees)
nasal_label <- textGrob("Nasal Cavity", rot = 90, gp = gpar(fontsize = 28, fontface = "bold"))
oral_label  <- textGrob("Oral Cavity", rot = 90, gp = gpar(fontsize = 28, fontface = "bold"))

# Arrange each row with two columns: label and plot
row1 <- arrangeGrob(nasal_label, top5_plot_nasal_g, ncol = 2, widths = c(0.1, 0.9))
row2 <- arrangeGrob(oral_label, top_plot_combined, ncol = 2, widths = c(0.1, 0.9))

# Combine rows into one figure
combined_plot_g <- grid.arrange(row1, row2, nrow = 2)
ggsave(
  filename    = "Top_6_Genera_Niches.tiff",
  plot        = combined_plot_g,
  device      = "tiff",
  dpi         = 300,
  width       = 13,      # in inches
  height      = 17,      # adjust as needed
  units       = "in",
  compression = "lzw"
)
```
